title: Text Preprocessing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of text cleaning, tokenization strategies, and vectorization for NLP pipelines.

questions.question: What does the '##' prefix mean in BERT's WordPiece tokenizer output (e.g., '##ness')?

options.text: The token is a stop word that should be ignored
The token is a hashtag from social media text
The token is a continuation subword — it follows directly from the previous token without a space
The token has low TF-IDF weight and will be masked during training

questions.question: Why does modern subword tokenization (BPE, WordPiece) solve a problem that word-level tokenization cannot?

options.text: Subword tokenization is much faster to compute
It eliminates the out-of-vocabulary problem — any unseen word can be split into known subword pieces
Subword tokenization removes stop words automatically
It converts text to lowercase before splitting

questions.question: In TF-IDF, a word that appears in almost every document in the corpus will receive a high or low IDF score?

options.text: High — very common words are the most important features
Low — IDF penalizes words that appear in many documents because they carry little discriminative information
Zero — TF-IDF does not score common words at all
It depends on the term frequency in the specific document

questions.question: When should you apply stop word removal (removing words like 'the', 'is', 'are')?

options.text: Always — stop words are never informative
For classical ML with bag-of-words or TF-IDF features; NOT for transformers, which need stop words for grammatical context
Only when training data has fewer than 1000 documents
Only for non-English languages

questions.question: What does the `attention_mask` tensor returned by a Hugging Face tokenizer indicate?

options.text: Which words the model should focus on for the target task
The importance weights assigned to each token by the self-attention mechanism
A binary mask (1 for real tokens, 0 for padding tokens) that tells the model which positions to process and which to ignore
The mask used by dropout regularization during training
